text: This paper describes a new release of Stanford CoreNLP, a Java annotated library for natural language processing (NLP) applications that provides a highly flexible analysis pipeline with a wide range of applications, from basic tokenization, part-of-speech tagging and naming entity recognition to fully specified parsing and coreference. We describe its underlying architecture, ease of use for developers, and individual annotators. We argue that the success of Stanford CoreNLP stems from focusing on a small set of applications with a simple, easy-to-use API and high-quality models for linguistics, without the overhead and structural burdens of a full development environment.
headline: Fully Human Written
